data.title: BridgeBio Buy Rating Reiterated as Analyst Sees Extended Patent Runway, Delayed Tafamidis Competition and Maintains $100 Price Target
data.description:
H.C. Wainwright analyst Ram Selvaraju reiterated a Buy rating on BridgeBio Pharma today and set a price target of $100.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ram Selvaraju has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to BridgeBio’s competitive positioning and pipeline momentum. He believes that potential settlements around generic versions of Pfizer’s tafamidis could push meaningful competition out to around 2032, which would support stronger and more durable pricing power for BridgeBio’s acoramidis franchise and improve its long-term revenue profile.

Selvaraju also highlights that acoramidis may enjoy patent protection into the late 2030s, providing an extended runway for growth that the market may not fully appreciate. In addition, he points to multiple upcoming regulatory catalysts, including New Drug Applications for ribitol and encaleret and the advancement of infigratinib in achondroplasia and hypochondroplasia, as drivers of future value creation, supporting his reiterated $100 price target and Buy recommendation.

In another report released on April 23, Piper Sandler also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $111.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy on BioNTech, Maintains $130 Price Target on Strength of Pumitamig Oncology Pipeline
data.description:
Robert Burns, an analyst from H.C. Wainwright, reiterated the Buy rating on BioNTech SE. The associated price target remains the same with $130.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Robert Burns has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors related to BioNTech’s oncology pipeline and competitive positioning. He views Merck’s MK-2010 bispecific antibody as an emerging agent with early activity, but not yet a major competitive danger to pumitamig, given MK-2010’s very small Phase 1 sample size and the limited clinical detail disclosed so far.

Burns emphasizes that pumitamig is already significantly ahead in development, supported by seven active Phase 3 studies in first-line non-small cell lung cancer across different PD-L1 expression settings and treatment combinations. Using a DCF framework with an 8% discount rate, 2% terminal growth, and a 28% tax rate, he derives a firm value of about $33B, which supports maintaining his unchanged 12‑month price target of $130 per share, while acknowledging clinical, regulatory, commercial, and dilution risks.

Burns covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Cardiff Oncology, Nurix Therapeutics, and Karyopharm Therapeutics. According to TipRanks, Burns has an average return of 22.6% and a 50.80% success rate on recommended stocks.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy on Santhera, Citing Leadership Transition and AGAMREE Momentum with Unchanged $30 Price Target
data.description:
Santhera Pharmaceuticals Holding, the Healthcare sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Ram Selvaraju from H.C. Wainwright reiterated a Buy rating on the stock and has a CHF30.00 price target.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ram Selvaraju has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors related to Santhera’s leadership and commercial trajectory. He emphasizes the appointment of Orlando Oliveira as CEO, noting Oliveira’s extensive global biopharma background, success in specialty and rare diseases, and demonstrated ability to scale organizations and execute value-creating transactions across multiple regions.

Selvaraju also highlights the solid platform established under outgoing CEO Dario Eklund, who repositioned Santhera into a focused commercial rare-disease company built around AGAMREE. With recent approvals of AGAMREE in the U.S., Europe, and the UK and launches underway, alongside strengthened finances and global partnerships, Selvaraju sees a favorable setup for continued revenue growth, supporting his reiterated Buy rating and unchanged $30 price target.

data.title: Alpha Cognition: Buy Rating Reaffirmed as Early Galantos Settlement Enhances Financial Flexibility; $18 Price Target Maintained
data.description:
Analyst Ram Selvaraju of H.C. Wainwright reiterated a Buy rating on Alpha Cognition Inc, retaining the price target of $18.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ram Selvaraju has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Alpha Cognition’s improved financial flexibility and streamlined obligations. The early settlement with Galantos eliminates future royalty and milestone payments on ZUNVEYL at an estimated ~40% discount to their market value, which simplifies the company’s future cash outflows and strengthens its long‑term economic position without materially weakening its current cash balance.

Selvaraju views the company as adequately funded through its projected cash flow break-even point, reducing financing risk and supporting his unchanged $18 price target. In addition, he highlights the appointment of experienced executive Bethany Sensenig to the board, whose track record in corporate finance, operations, and large-scale transactions is expected to bolster Alpha Cognition’s strategic execution and governance, further underpinning the favorable risk‑reward profile he sees in the shares.

In another report released on April 17, Craig-Hallum also initiated coverage with a Buy rating on the stock with a $14.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Marc Bianchi Reiterates Buy on Schlumberger, Lifts Price Target to $66 on Resilient Results and Long-Term Growth Drivers
data.description:
Marc Bianchi, an analyst from TD Cowen, maintained the Buy rating on Schlumberger. The associated price target is $66.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Marc Bianchi has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight Schlumberger’s resilience and upside potential despite war-related disruptions. He notes that underlying first-quarter results essentially matched the company’s original guidance once conflict impacts are adjusted out, and he sees second-quarter performance tracking prior expectations with room for improvement in the second half as global oilfield services spending expands.

He also points to the company’s growing digital and data center solutions businesses, which are delivering solid double-digit growth and should contribute meaningfully to revenue and profitability over time. Incorporating slightly stronger long-term pricing and growth assumptions, his discounted cash flow analysis supports a higher price target of $66, reinforcing the conviction that the stock remains attractively valued at current levels.

data.title: Analyst Reaffirms Buy on Waste Connections, Keeps $200 Price Target Unchanged Amid Margin Upside and Tech-Driven Catalysts
data.description:
Analyst James Schumm of TD Cowen reiterated a Buy rating on Waste Connections, retaining the price target of $200.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

James Schumm has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Waste Connections’ earnings trajectory and margin outlook. He views current modeling for 2026 as conservative, noting that margins are already trending higher while the market has grown overly pessimistic on the waste sector, leading to compressed valuations despite improving fundamentals.

He also highlights multiple catalysts that can lift profitability beyond traditional price‑cost levers, including AI‑driven pricing optimization, call‑center digitization, and dynamic truck routing, which together could add roughly $100 million of EBITDA and about 100 basis points of margin over time. In addition, solid special‑waste volume growth and indications that it may foreshadow a recovery in construction and demolition activity support a favorable demand backdrop, leading him to reaffirm his Buy rating and keep the $200 price target unchanged.

In another report released on April 24, ATB Cormark Capital Markets also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a C$305.00 price target.
